description_long: This object is a classic submachine gun, specifically resembling the Thompson M1928, known for its iconic use during the early to mid-20th century. It features a wooden fixed stock and foregrip, both with a rich brown finish, and a black metal receiver and barrel assembly. The rifle is equipped with a large, round drum magazine mounted beneath the receiver, characteristic of the Thompson design. The barrel is long and slender, ending in a simple muzzle with no visible attachments. Iron sights are present on the top of the receiver and barrel for aiming. The overall construction combines wood and metal, giving it a robust and vintage appearance suitable for historical or urban settings.
description: A vintage Thompson-style submachine gun with a wooden stock and foregrip, black metal body, and a prominent drum magazine.
description_view_0: Front view showing the circular drum magazine and the alignment of the barrel and sights.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the wooden stock, foregrip, long barrel, and the full profile of the drum magazine.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the stock and the circular end of the drum magazine.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the same features as view 1, with the wooden elements and drum magazine clearly visible.
